issues: The draft calls this the 'championship game' but the OCR clearly describes a round-robin tournament game, not the final. The Centennial Cup was a week-long round-robin format and this was just Richmond's first game.
The headline 'Sockeyes Win Centennial Cup' is fabricated — the OCR contains no such headline; this is a game report within the round-robin, not a championship-clinching win.

The long description says 'to win the Centennial Cup junior-A hockey championship' which is factually wrong — this was a round-robin pool game, not the final. Richmond went on to win the Cup on May 9.
The short description says 'championship game' which is incorrect for the same reason.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Jason Phillips scored three goals as the Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Dartmouth Fuel Kids 7-3 in a Centennial Cup round-robin game in Humboldt, Saskatchewan on May 3, 1987.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Jason Phillips scored three goals to lead the Richmond Sockeyes to a 7-3 win over the Dartmouth Fuel Kids in a Centennial Cup junior-A round-robin game in Humboldt, Saskatchewan on May 3, 1987. Bryon Moller, Steve Jaques, Dave Tomlinson, and Matt Hervey also scored for Richmond, who outshot Dartmouth 44-29 and led 3-1 after the first period and 6-2 after the second. It was Dartmouth's second loss in two days, having also fallen 7-2 to host Humboldt Broncos. Richmond was scheduled to face the Pembroke Lumber Kings (Ontario) in their next round-robin game.
review.notes: The AI draft critically mischaracterizes this as the championship final, when it is clearly a round-robin pool game played May 3 — six days before the actual Centennial Cup final on May 9. No headline is discernible in the OCR; the article appears to be a short wire-service game report. Headline field has been cleared to avoid fabrication.
